job_statement: Install the OpenShift GitOps Operator using the web console so I can set up GitOps capabilities in my OpenShift cluster
situation: Cluster administrator needs to enable GitOps functionality
motivation: Enable declarative application deployment and cluster resource management using Argo CD

alternatives_considered: CLI installation method, manual Argo CD installation, community Argo CD Operator

notes: Source module: modules/installing-gitops-operator-in-web-console.adoc (PROCEDURE). Covers web console installation with OperatorHub. Requires cluster-admin role and Marketplace capability.

job_statement: Select the appropriate update channel and version for OpenShift GitOps so I can control which version of the Operator is installed
situation: Installing OpenShift GitOps Operator and need to choose between latest or specific version channel
motivation: Ensure compatibility with cluster version and control upgrade timing
expected_outcome: Correct version of GitOps Operator is installed from the selected channel
alternatives_considered: Using default latest channel, pinning to specific version channel like gitops-1.8

notes: Latest channel is default and recommended. Specific version channels like gitops-1.8 available for version control. Channel selection happens during installation.

job_statement: Choose the installation namespace for OpenShift GitOps Operator so I can organize operator resources appropriately
situation: Installing the Operator and selecting where it should be deployed
motivation: Follow best practices for operator namespace organization and enable cluster monitoring
expected_outcome: Operator is installed in the correct namespace with monitoring enabled if desired
evidence: installing-openshift-gitops-reduced.adoc -> Section 'Installing {gitops-title} Operator in web console', lines 37-46 [module: modules/installing-gitops-operator-in-web-console.adoc, type: PROCEDURE]
notes: Default changed from openshift-operators to openshift-gitops-operator in version 1.10. Can enable cluster monitoring via checkbox or label.

job_statement: Verify that the OpenShift GitOps Operator installed successfully so I can confirm it is ready to use

job_statement: Create the required namespace and OperatorGroup for GitOps Operator so I can prepare the cluster for Operator installation

job_statement: Create and apply a Subscription resource for the GitOps Operator so I can trigger the installation from the Operator catalog
situation: Namespace and OperatorGroup are configured, ready to install Operator
motivation: Subscribe to the GitOps Operator from the Red Hat catalog to initiate installation

notes: Subscription specifies channel, installPlanApproval, source catalog, and namespace. Uses redhat-operators catalog from openshift-marketplace.

job_statement: Verify that GitOps Operator pods are running after CLI installation so I can confirm successful deployment
situation: After applying Subscription resource and waiting for installation to complete
motivation: Ensure all required components are running before using GitOps features
expected_outcome: All pods in openshift-gitops and openshift-gitops-operator namespaces show Running status

notes: Two namespaces to check: openshift-gitops (Argo CD components) and openshift-gitops-operator (operator controller). Multiple pods expected including application-controller, applicationset-controller, dex-server, redis, repo-server, and server.
